evals.prompt: Tenho 3 papers sobre hepatorenal syndrome (CONFIRM trial PMID 34233093, ATTIRE trial PMID 34388394, e o guideline EASL 2023). Quero: 1) Criar notebook 'HRS Concurso' e ingerir os 3 papers 2) Gerar quiz com 10 questoes difficulty hard focado em criterios diagnosticos e manejo 3) Gerar flashcards difficulty medium sobre os numeros-chave dos trials 4) Gerar podcast formato deep_dive para ouvir na corrida 5) Baixar o podcast como mp3. Usar MCP tools se disponiveis, senao CLI.
evals.expected_output: Complete command sequence: create notebook, alias, add 3 sources via PubMed URLs, quiz with --count 10 --difficulty 3 --focus (clinical content), flashcards --difficulty medium --focus (trial numbers), audio --format deep_dive --confirm, download mp3. All with --confirm, using alias throughout.
expectations: Creates notebook with descriptive medical title (not generic 'Untitled')
Adds all 3 sources using PubMed URLs (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/{PMID}/) or DOIs — not hallucinated URLs
Generates quiz with --count 10 --difficulty 3 AND --focus containing clinical terminology (diagnostic criteria, management, etc.)
Generates flashcards with --difficulty medium AND --focus mentioning trial endpoints or numbers
Generates audio with --format deep_dive --confirm
Sets an alias early and uses it throughout (avoids repeating UUID)
Downloads audio to .mp3 file with meaningful filename
Captures IDs from each creation step for use in subsequent steps (e.g., studio status before download)
Uses --confirm on every generation command

evals.prompt: Preciso montar um notebook de estudo aprofundado sobre TIPS (Transjugular Intrahepatic Portosystemic Shunt) -- indicacoes, contraindicacoes, e o impacto na sobrevida. Pipeline completo: 1) Criar notebook 'TIPS Deep Dive' 2) Rodar deep research para encontrar fontes atuais (guidelines + trials) 3) Importar as fontes encontradas 4) Gerar Study Guide report 5) Query: 'Quais as contraindicacoes absolutas vs relativas do TIPS segundo AASLD?' 6) Gerar audio debate format pra ouvir
evals.expected_output: Sequential pipeline: create notebook, research start --mode deep --notebook-id, research status (poll), research import, report create --format 'Study Guide', notebook query (one-shot), audio create --format debate. Proper sequencing with status checks.
expectations: Creates notebook with topic-descriptive title
Uses research start with --mode deep AND --notebook-id flag (mandatory)
Polls research status or waits before attempting import (proper sequencing)
Imports research results with research import command
Generates report with --format "Study Guide" (exact string with capital S and G and space)
Uses notebook query (NOT chat start) for the clinical question
Generates audio with --format debate --confirm
Checks studio status between generation requests
Does NOT hallucinate nlm commands that do not exist in v0.5.1

evals.prompt: Estou criando notebooks por especialidade para o concurso R3. Ja tenho: 'Hepatologia Concurso' (alias: hepato), 'Cardio Concurso' (alias: cardio), 'Nefro Concurso' (alias: nefro). Quero: 1) Adicionar uma URL em todos: https://www.nejm.org/doi/full/10.1056/NEJMra2310713 2) Gerar flashcards difficulty hard em todos os 3 notebooks 3) Cross-notebook query: 'Compare o manejo de hipertensao portal entre especialidades' 4) Taguear todos com 'concurso,r3,2026'
evals.expected_output: Efficient batch workflow: batch add-source (not 3x individual), batch studio for flashcards, cross query for comparison, tag add with --tags. All using aliases, all with --confirm.
expectations: Uses batch add-source (not 3 individual source add commands) for efficiency
Uses batch studio --type flashcards for all 3 notebooks (not individual creates)
Uses nlm cross query for the comparative question across notebooks
Uses tag add command with correct --tags syntax for all 3 notebooks
References aliases (hepato, cardio, nefro) instead of placeholder UUIDs
Includes --confirm on batch operations
Does NOT use nlm chat start for the cross-notebook question
